twofa.backup_title: Backup codes — store safely, each usable once:

license.title: License
license.upgrade_hint: Community limits active (1 org, 5 users, no SSO/export/white-label). A commercial license unlocks more.

items.a: A multi-tenant gateway that routes your requests to many AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, local models) through one OpenAI-compatible interface — with cost control, budgets and governance.
Use your username and password on the login page. If two-factor authentication is enabled, you also enter a 6-digit code from your authenticator app.

items.q: How do I enable two-factor authentication (2FA)?
items.a: Open Settings, choose Enable 2FA, scan the QR code with an authenticator app, enter the code and store your backup codes somewhere safe.

items.q: How do I create an API token?
items.a: Go to Tokens and select New token. The token works with any OpenAI-compatible client against the gateway /v1 endpoint.

items.q: How do I add an AI provider?
items.a: Go to Providers, choose a preset or a custom endpoint, enter the API key and the allowed models. Pick External for purchased APIs or Self-hosted for your own hardware.

items.q: What is the difference between external and self-hosted cost?
items.a: External providers are billed per token. Self-hosted providers are costed from electricity, hardware amortization and maintenance — both appear together in one bill in euro.

A budget sets a euro limit for an organization, group, user or token with a reset cycle (daily, weekly, monthly). When it runs out, the chosen action applies: block, warn or downgrade to a cheaper or local model.
The Costs page shows spend split into external versus self-hosted, with a time filter and CSV export (the export requires a commercial license).
Organizations are tenants — separate companies or teams. Each user belongs to one organization, and an organization admins only see their own organization data.
The whole interface is available in 12 languages. Switch language with the selector in the sidebar.

items.q: What is the difference between the free and the commercial version?
items.a: The free Community tier covers basic use (one organization, up to five users). Commercial features (more organizations and users, SSO, cost export, white-label) require a paid license.
The gateway runs on your own server (self-hosted). With data governance you can redact sensitive data before it reaches external models and keep a full audit trail.
